options: Cucumber/Gherkin .feature files (matches haiku-method's existing pattern in .haiku/features/)
Vitest unit-style behavioral tests only (no Gherkin)
Mixed: Gherkin for SPA workflows + Vitest for backend contract tests
decision_log.choice: Cucumber/Gherkin .feature files (matches haiku-method's existing pattern in .haiku/features/)
decision_log.rationale: The behavioral-spec discovery already produced 5 .feature files in .haiku/intents/{slug}/features/ following the project's existing convention. Sticking with Gherkin keeps acceptance criteria readable to non-engineers and matches universal-feedback intent's pattern. Backend contract tests in DATA-CONTRACTS.md still get Vitest equivalents at development time.
